title: erica viridiflora subsp. redacta
intro: Erica viridiflora subsp. redacta is a subspecies of flowering plant in the heath family, Erica, native to the Azores Islands, characterized by its vibrant green flowers. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Erica viridiflora subsp. redacta include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant activity, which can contribute to overall well-being. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tincture or infusion, and it can also be dried and used in teas, salves, and creams to harness its medicinal properties.
